summary: The diff implements most requirements well: a dedicated /admin route with authentication/role checks (REQ-1), full CRUD backend functions for episodes, characters, and content (REQ-2, REQ-3, REQ-6), corresponding frontend management panels (REQ-4, REQ-5, REQ-7), and a dark-themed sidebar dashboard (REQ-9). The primary gap is REQ-8, which requires a dedicated migration.mo file to initialise new stable variables with empty defaults and preserve existing data across canister upgrades — this file is not present in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Migrate stable backend state to include new episodes, characters, and general content stable variables; generate migration.mo that initialises new stable vars with empty defaults; ensure existing contact request and admin role data is preserved during canister upgrade.
missing_requirements.reason: No migration.mo file appears anywhere in the diff. The AC explicitly requires 'migration.mo is generated that initialises new stable vars with empty defaults'. Additionally, the backend/main.mo snippet uses Map.empty assignments rather than stable var declarations in the visible portion, which may not persist across upgrades, though the file is truncated.

hallucinated_features.description: CSV export functionality for contact requests
hallucinated_features.reason: The AdminContactRequestsPanel includes a full CSV export feature (handleExport function, Download button, blob/URL creation) that was not requested in any requirement. The build request only asks for viewing and managing contact requests.

hallucinated_features.description: User profile management functions added to backend
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend/main.mo includes UserProfile type and getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile functions that were not requested in any of the REQ-1 through REQ-9 requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: Admin Dashboard link added to TopNav for authenticated admin users
hallucinated_features.reason: The TopNav was modified to show a conditional Dashboard link to /admin for authenticated admins. This modifies the public-facing navigation component and was not specified in any requirement.
